title: Three-turn state return-loss trajectory
question: Within that cohort, what percentage of realized revenue was lost in each state?
history: Compare California and New York realized revenue last quarter.
Which state lost more revenue to returns within that cohort?
canonical_sql: SELECT u.state, ROUND(SUM(IF(oi.status = 'Returned', oi.sale_price, 0)), 2) AS return_loss, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(IF(oi.status = 'Returned', oi.sale_price, 0)), SUM(IF(oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned'), oi.sale_price, 0))), 3) AS loss_rate FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE u.state IN ('California', 'New York') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', QUARTER), INTERVAL 1 QUARTER) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', QUARTER) GROUP BY u.state ORDER BY loss_rate DESC, u.state

replay.candidate_sql: SELECT u.state, ROUND(SUM(IF(oi.status = 'Returned', oi.sale_price, 0)), 2) AS return_loss, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(IF(oi.status = 'Returned', oi.sale_price, 0)), SUM(IF(oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned'), oi.sale_price, 0))), 3) AS loss_rate FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE u.state IN ('California', 'New York') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', QUARTER), INTERVAL 1 QUARTER) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', QUARTER) GROUP BY u.state ORDER BY loss_rate DESC, u.state

report.question: Within that cohort, what percentage of realized revenue was lost in each state?
report.answer: New York's loss rate was 3% versus California's 2% within the retained cohort.
report.sql: SELECT u.state, ROUND(SUM(IF(oi.status = 'Returned', oi.sale_price, 0)), 2) AS return_loss, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(IF(oi.status = 'Returned', oi.sale_price, 0)), SUM(IF(oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned'), oi.sale_price, 0))), 3) AS loss_rate FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE u.state IN ('California', 'New York') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', QUARTER), INTERVAL 1 QUARTER) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', QUARTER) GROUP BY u.state ORDER BY loss_rate DESC, u.state

history_turns.question: Compare California and New York realized revenue last quarter.
Which state lost more revenue to returns within that cohort?

title: Added threshold and prior-month comparison
question: Compare the winning category with the month before.
history: Show the top categories last complete month.
Only keep categories with at least 100 distinct orders.
canonical_sql: SELECT p.category, DATE_TRUNC(DATE(oi.created_at), MONTH) AS month, COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) AS orders, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH), INTERVAL 2 MONTH) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH) GROUP BY p.category, month HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) >= 100 ORDER BY revenue DESC, p.category, month

replay.candidate_sql: SELECT p.category, DATE_TRUNC(DATE(oi.created_at), MONTH) AS month, COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) AS orders, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH), INTERVAL 2 MONTH) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH) GROUP BY p.category, month HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) >= 100 ORDER BY revenue DESC, p.category, month

report.question: Compare the winning category with the month before.
report.answer: Outerwear & Coats rose from $45,000 to $50,000 while retaining the minimum-order threshold.
report.sql: SELECT p.category, DATE_TRUNC(DATE(oi.created_at), MONTH) AS month, COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) AS orders, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH), INTERVAL 2 MONTH) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH) GROUP BY p.category, month HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) >= 100 ORDER BY revenue DESC, p.category, month

history_turns.question: Show the top categories last complete month.
Only keep categories with at least 100 distinct orders.

title: Latest correction replaces gross-sales intent
question: Now rank categories using the corrected measure.
history: Rank categories by gross sales.
Correction: use net realized sales and exclude cancelled and returned items.
canonical_sql: SELECT p.category, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS net_realized_sales FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY net_realized_sales DESC LIMIT 10

replay.candidate_sql: SELECT p.category, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS net_realized_sales FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY net_realized_sales DESC LIMIT 10

report.question: Now rank categories using the corrected measure.
report.answer: Outerwear & Coats led corrected net realized sales with $90,000.
report.sql: SELECT p.category, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS net_realized_sales FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY net_realized_sales DESC LIMIT 10

history_turns.question: Rank categories by gross sales.
Correction: use net realized sales and exclude cancelled and returned items.

title: Long history retains four constraints
question: Run the final comparison with every constraint retained.
history: Use New York customers.
Use realized statuses only.
Use the last complete month.
Require at least 50 items per category.
canonical_sql: SELECT p.category, COUNT(*) AS items, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE u.state = 'New York' AND oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH), INTERVAL 1 MONTH) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH) GROUP BY p.category HAVING COUNT(*) >= 50 ORDER BY revenue DESC

replay.candidate_sql: SELECT p.category, COUNT(*) AS items, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE u.state = 'New York' AND oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH), INTERVAL 1 MONTH) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH) GROUP BY p.category HAVING COUNT(*) >= 50 ORDER BY revenue DESC

report.question: Run the final comparison with every constraint retained.
report.answer: Outerwear & Coats led with $30,000 across 75 items under all retained constraints.
report.sql: SELECT p.category, COUNT(*) AS items, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE u.state = 'New York' AND oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH), INTERVAL 1 MONTH) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH) GROUP BY p.category HAVING COUNT(*) >= 50 ORDER BY revenue DESC

title: Topic switch clears stale category scope
question: How many repeat customers do we have instead?
history: Show Outerwear revenue last month.
Break that category down by product.
canonical_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS repeat_customers FROM ( SELECT user_id FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.orders` WHERE status = 'Complete' GROUP BY user_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) > 1 )

Show their last complete month revenue.
